input.description: Explore project structure and FastAPI setup
input.prompt:

4. Any existing rate limiting or IP-related code

7. Any existing dependencies (especially relevant ones like redis, starlette, etc.)

Be thorough - read the actual file contents, not just file names. I need to understand the existing patterns to plan a new middleware implementation.

1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["pdm-backend"]
3	build-backend = "pdm.backend"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "fastapi"
7	dynamic = ["version"]
8	description = "FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production"
9	readme = "README.md"
10	license = "MIT"
11	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
12	requires-python = ">=3.10"
13	authors = [
14	    { name = "Sebastián Ramírez", email = "tiangolo@gmail.com" },
15	]
16	classifiers = [
17	    "Intended Audience :: Information Technology",
18	    "Intended Audience :: System Administrators",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python",
22	    "Topic :: Internet",
23	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks",
24	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
25	    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries",
26	    "Topic :: Software Development",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
29	    "Environment :: Web Environment",
30	    "Framework :: AsyncIO",
31	    "Framework :: FastAPI",
32	    "Framework :: Pydantic",
33	    "Framework :: Pydantic :: 2",
34	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
35	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only",
36	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
37	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
38	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
39	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
40	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
41	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP :: HTTP Servers",
42	    "Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP",
43	]
44	dependencies = [
45	    "starlette>=0.46.0",
46	    "pydantic>=2.9.0",
47	    "typing-extensions>=4.8.0",
48	    "typing-inspection>=0.4.2",
49	    "annotated-doc>=0.0.2",
50	]
51	
52	[project.urls]
53	Homepage = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
54	Documentation = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/"
55	Repository = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi"
56	Issues = "https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues"
57	Changelog = "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/release-notes/"
58	
59	[project.optional-dependencies]
60	standard = [
61	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
62	    # For the test client
63	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
64	    # For templates
65	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
66	    # For forms and file uploads
67	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
68	    # To validate email fields
69	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
70	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
71	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
72	    # # Settings management
73	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
74	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
75	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
76	]
77	
78	standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli = [
79	    "fastapi-cli[standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli] >=0.0.8",
80	    # For the test client
81	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
82	    # For templates
83	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
84	    # For forms and file uploads
85	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
86	    # To validate email fields
87	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
88	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
89	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
90	    # # Settings management
91	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
92	    # # Extra Pydantic data types
93	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
94	]
95	
96	all = [
97	    "fastapi-cli[standard] >=0.0.8",
98	    # # For the test client
99	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
100	    # For templates
101	    "jinja2 >=3.1.5",
102	    # For forms and file uploads
103	    "python-multipart >=0.0.18",
104	    # For Starlette's SessionMiddleware, not commonly used with FastAPI
105	    "itsdangerous >=1.1.0",
106	    # For Starlette's schema generation, would not be used with FastAPI
107	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1",
108	    # To validate email fields
109	    "email-validator >=2.0.0",
110	    # Uvicorn with uvloop
111	    "uvicorn[standard] >=0.12.0",
112	    # Settings management
113	    "pydantic-settings >=2.0.0",
114	    # Extra Pydantic data types
115	    "pydantic-extra-types >=2.0.0",
116	]
117	
118	[project.scripts]
119	fastapi = "fastapi.cli:main"
120	
121	[dependency-groups]
122	dev = [
123	    { include-group = "tests" },
124	    { include-group = "docs" },
125	    { include-group = "translations" },
126	    "playwright >=1.57.0",
127	    "prek >=0.2.22",
128	]
129	docs = [
130	    { include-group = "docs-tests" },
131	    "black >=25.1.0",
132	    "cairosvg >=2.8.2",
133	    # for MkDocs live reload
134	    "click==8.2.1",
135	    "griffe-typingdoc >=0.3.0",
136	    "griffe-warnings-deprecated >=1.1.0",
137	    "jieba >=0.42.1",
138	    "markdown-include-variants >=0.0.8",
139	    "mdx-include >=1.4.1,<2.0.0",
140	    "mkdocs-macros-plugin >=1.5.0",
141	    "mkdocs-material >=9.7.0",
142	    "mkdocs-redirects >=1.2.1,<1.3.0",
143	    "mkdocstrings[python] >=0.30.1",
144	    "pillow >=11.3.0",
145	    "python-slugify >=8.0.4",
146	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
147	    "typer >=0.21.1",
148	]
149	docs-tests = [
150	    "httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0",
151	    "ruff >=0.14.14",
152	    # For UJSONResponse
153	    "ujson >=5.8.0",
154	    # For ORJSONResponse
155	    "orjson >=3.9.3",
156	]
157	github-actions = [
158	    "httpx >=0.27.0,<1.0.0",
159	    "pydantic >=2.9.0,<3.0.0",
160	    "pydantic-settings >=2.1.0,<3.0.0",
161	    "pygithub >=2.3.0,<3.0.0",
162	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
163	    "smokeshow >=0.5.0",
164	]
165	tests = [
166	    { include-group = "docs-tests" },
167	    "anyio[trio] >=3.2.1,<5.0.0",
168	    "coverage[toml] >=7.13,<8.0",
169	    "dirty-equals >=0.9.0",
170	    "flask >=3.0.0,<4.0.0",
171	    "inline-snapshot >=0.21.1",
172	    "mypy >=1.14.1",
173	    "pwdlib[argon2] >=0.2.1",
174	    "pyjwt >=2.9.0",
175	    "pytest >=9.0.0",
176	    "pytest-codspeed >=4.2.0",
177	    "pyyaml >=5.3.1,<7.0.0",
178	    "sqlmodel >=0.0.31",
179	    "strawberry-graphql >=0.200.0,<1.0.0",
180	    "ty>=0.0.9",
181	    "types-orjson >=3.6.2",
182	    "types-ujson >=5.10.0.20240515",
183	    "a2wsgi >=1.9.0,<=2.0.0",
184	    "pytest-xdist[psutil]>=2.5.0",
185	    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
186	    "pytest-sugar>=1.0.0",
187	    "pytest-timeout>=2.4.0",
188	]
189	translations = [
190	    "gitpython >=3.1.46",
191	    "pydantic-ai >=0.4.10",
192	    "pygithub >=2.8.1",
193	]
194	
195	[tool.pdm]
196	version = { source = "file", path = "fastapi/__init__.py" }
197	distribution = true
198	
199	[tool.pdm.build]
200	source-includes = [
201	    "tests/",
202	    "docs_src/",
203	    "scripts/",
204	    # For a test
205	    "docs/en/docs/img/favicon.png",
206	]
207	
208	[tool.mypy]
209	plugins = ["pydantic.mypy"]
210	strict = true
211	
212	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
213	module = "fastapi.concurrency"
214	warn_unused_ignores = false
215	ignore_missing_imports = true
216	
217	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
218	module = "fastapi.tests.*"
219	ignore_missing_imports = true
220	check_untyped_defs = true
221	
222	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
223	module = "docs_src.*"
224	disallow_incomplete_defs = false
225	disallow_untyped_defs = false
226	disallow_untyped_calls = false
227	
228	[tool.pytest]
229	minversion = "9.0"
230	addopts = [
231	  "--strict-config",
232	  "--strict-markers",
233	  "--ignore=docs_src",
234	]
235	strict_xfail = true
236	filterwarnings = [
237	    "error",
238	]
239	timeout = "20"
240	
241	[tool.coverage.run]
242	parallel = true
243	data_file = "coverage/.coverage"
244	source = [
245	    "docs_src",
246	    "tests",
247	    "fastapi"
248	]
249	relative_files = true
250	context = '${CONTEXT}'
251	omit = [
252	    "tests/benchmarks/*",
253	    "docs_src/response_model/tutorial003_04_py39.py",
254	    "docs_src/response_model/tutorial003_04_py310.py",
255	    "docs_src/dependencies/tutorial013_an_py310.py",  # temporary code example?
256	    "docs_src/dependencies/tutorial014_an_py310.py",  # temporary code example?
257	    # Pydantic v1 migration, no longer tested
258	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial001_an_py310.py",
259	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial002_an_py310.py",
260	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial003_an_py310.py",
261	    "docs_src/pydantic_v1_in_v2/tutorial004_an_py310.py",
262	]
263	
264	[tool.coverage.report]
265	show_missing = true
266	sort = "-Cover"
267	
268	[tool.coverage.html]
269	show_contexts = true
270	
271	[tool.ruff.lint]
272	select = [
273	    "E",  # pycodestyle errors
274	    "W",  # pycodestyle warnings
275	    "F",  # pyflakes
276	    "I",  # isort
277	    "B",  # flake8-bugbear
278	    "C4",  # flake8-comprehensions
279	    "UP",  # pyupgrade
280	]
281	ignore = [
282	    "E501",  # line too long, handled by black
283	    "B008",  # do not perform function calls in argument defaults
284	    "C901",  # too complex
285	    "W191",  # indentation contains tabs
286	]
287	
288	[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
289	"__init__.py" = ["F401"]
290	"docs_src/custom_request_and_route/tutorial002_an_py310.py" = ["B904"]
291	"docs_src/custom_request_and_route/tutorial002_an_py39.py" = ["B904"]
292	"docs_src/custom_request_and_route/tutorial002_py310.py" = ["B904"]
293	"docs_src/custom_request_and_route/tutorial002_py39.py" = ["B904"]
294	"docs_src/custom_response/tutorial007_py310.py" = ["B007"]
295	"docs_src/custom_response/tutorial007_py39.py" = ["B007"]

8	@pytest.fixture
9	def app():
10	    app = FastAPI()
11	    app.add_middleware(
12	        RateLimitMiddleware,
13	        requests_per_minute=60,
14	        burst_size=5,
15	    )
16	
17	    @app.get("/")
18	    async def root():
19	        return {"message": "hello"}

33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54

3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send
4	
5	
6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

2	import pytest
3	from fastapi import FastAPI
4	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
5	from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware
6	
7	
8	@pytest.fixture
9	def app():
10	    app = FastAPI()
11	    app.add_middleware(
12	        RateLimitMiddleware,
13	        requests_per_minute=60,
14	        burst_size=5,
15	    )
16	
17	    @app.get("/")
18	    async def root():
19	        return {"message": "hello"}
20	
21	    @app.get("/other")
22	    async def other():
23	        return {"message": "other"}
24	
25	    return app
26	
27	
28	@pytest.fixture
29	def client(app):
30	    return TestClient(app)
31	
32	
33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

4	from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
5	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
6	
7	app = FastAPI()
8	
9	router = APIRouter()
10	
11	
12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55	
56	@router.post("/middleware")
57	def run_middleware(file: UploadFile = File(..., description="Big File")):
58	    return {"message": "OK"}
59	
60	
61	app.include_router(router)
62	app.add_middleware(ContentSizeLimitMiddleware, max_content_size=2**8)
63	
64	
65	client = TestClient(app)
66

68	def test_custom_middleware_exception(tmp_path: Path):
69	    default_pydantic_max_size = 2**16
70	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"
71	    path.write_bytes(b"x" * (default_pydantic_max_size + 1))
72	
73	    with client:
74	        with open(path, "rb") as file:
75	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
76	        assert response.status_code == 422, response.text
77	        assert response.json() == {
78	            "detail": {
79	                "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
80	                "code": 999,
81	                "message": "File limit exceeded",
82	            }
83	        }
84

86	def test_custom_middleware_exception_not_raised(tmp_path: Path):
87	    path = tmp_path / "test.txt"

89	
90	    with client:

92	            response = client.post("/middleware", files={"file": file})
93	        assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
94	        assert response.json() == {"message": "OK"}

11	class Session:

24	    raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail="Exception after yield")

37

3	from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive exploration report.

## Exploration Report: FastAPI Codebase Testing Infrastructure

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Directory Layout:**
- `/fastapi/` - Main library code
- `/tests/` - Test suite (215+ test files)
- `/fastapi/middleware/` - Middleware implementations
- `/pyproject.toml` - Project configuration and test settings

### 2. TEST CLIENT SETUP & PATTERNS

#### TestClient Import & Usage
```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
```

**Basic Setup Pattern** (from test_rate_limiter.py):
```python
@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    # ... configure app
    return app

@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)
```

**Common Usage:**
- `client.get(path, headers={...})` - GET requests
- `client.post(path, ...)` - POST requests
- Response methods: `response.status_code`, `response.json()`, `response.headers`
- TestClient handles httpx automatically for ASGI testing

#### Key TestClient Features Used in Tests:
- **raise_server_exceptions** parameter: `TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False)` to catch 500 errors without raising
- **headers parameter**: `client.get("/path", headers={"header-name": "value"})`
- **files parameter**: `client.post("/path", files={"file": file})`
- **Response object properties**:
  - `response.status_code`
  - `response.json()` - Parse response body
  - `response.headers` - Access response headers (case-insensitive dict-like)
  - `response.text` - Raw text response

### 3. PYTEST FIXTURES & PATTERNS

**Fixture Patterns Observed:**

1. **Simple Fixture Pattern** (test_dependency_cache.py):
```python
app = FastAPI()

# ... app setup code ...

client = TestClient(app)

def test_name():
    response = client.get("/path")
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

2. **Parametrized Fixture Pattern** (test_request_params/test_header/):
```python
@pytest.mark.parametrize("path", ["/path1", "/path2"])
def test_name(path: str):
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get(path, headers={"p": "value"})
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

3. **Class-Based Test Organization** (test_rate_limiter.py):
```python
@pytest.fixture
def app():
    return FastAPI()

@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)

class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_method(self, client):
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 200
```

4. **Setup/Teardown Pattern** (test_rate_limiter.py):
```python
def test_custom_limits(self):
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware, ...)
    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"ok": True}
    client = TestClient(app)
    # test logic
```

5. **Dependency Injection Pattern**:
```python
@pytest.fixture(name="runner")
def get_runner():
    runner = CliRunner()
    with runner.isolated_filesystem():
        yield runner
```

### 4. CONFTEST.PY & TEST CONFIGURATION

**Found conftest.py location:**
- `/scripts/tests/test_translation_fixer/conftest.py`

**No root-level conftest.py in /tests/** - Tests use inline fixtures instead

**Fixture Patterns from conftest.py:**
```python
@pytest.fixture(name="runner")
def get_runner():
    runner = CliRunner()
    with runner.isolated_filesystem():
        yield runner

def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items: list[pytest.Item]) -> None:
    # Hooks for test collection modification
    if sys.platform != "win32":
        return
    for item in items:
        item_path = Path(item.fspath).resolve()
        if item_path.is_relative_to(THIS_DIR):
            item.add_marker(skip_on_windows)
```

### 5. PYTEST CONFIGURATION

**From pyproject.toml `[tool.pytest]`:**
```toml
minversion = "9.0"
addopts = [
  "--strict-config",
  "--strict-markers",
  "--ignore=docs_src",
]
strict_xfail = true
filterwarnings = [
    "error",  # Treat warnings as errors
]
timeout = "20"  # 20-second test timeout
```

**Test Dependencies:**
```toml
tests = [
    "pytest >=9.0.0",
    "pytest-codspeed >=4.2.0",
    "coverage[toml] >=7.13,<8.0",
    "dirty-equals >=0.9.0",
    "inline-snapshot >=0.21.1",
    "anyio[trio] >=3.2.1,<5.0.0",
    "pytest-xdist[psutil]>=2.5.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.0.0",
    "pytest-sugar>=1.0.0",
    "pytest-timeout>=2.4.0",
]
```

### 6. REQUEST/RESPONSE HEADERS HANDLING

#### Header Assertions in Tests:

**Response Headers Access** (test_repeated_cookie_headers.py):
```python
direct_response = client.get("/directCookie")
assert direct_response.headers["set-cookie"] == indirect_response.headers["set-cookie"]
```

**Header Passing in Requests** (test_request_params/test_header/test_required_str.py):
```python
response = client.get(path, headers={"p": "hello"})
assert response.status_code == 200
```

**Rate Limit Headers** (test_rate_limiter.py):
```python
response = client.get("/")
assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
remaining = int(response.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
```

**Header Value Assertions:**
```python
assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
```

### 7. EXISTING MIDDLEWARE IMPLEMENTATIONS

**FastAPI Middleware Directory Structure:**
```
/fastapi/middleware/
├── __init__.py         # Re-exports from Starlette
├── asyncexitstack.py   # Custom async context manager
├── cors.py            # CORS (imported from Starlette)
├── gzip.py            # GZip (imported from Starlette)
├── httpsredirect.py   # HTTPS redirect (imported from Starlette)
├── trustedhost.py     # Trusted host (imported from Starlette)
└── wsgi.py            # WSGI (imported from Starlette)
```

**Custom Middleware Pattern** (asyncexitstack.py):
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

**Middleware Registration:**
```python
app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware, requests_per_minute=60, burst_size=5)
```

**Custom Exception Middleware** (test_custom_middleware_exception.py):
```python
class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
        self.app = app
        self.max_content_size = max_content_size

    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        # ... custom logic
```

### 8. FASTAPI APPLICATION CONFIGURATION

**TestClient Options:**
- `raise_server_exceptions=False` - Catch 500 errors without raising

**App Configuration Patterns:**
```python
app = FastAPI(openapi_external_docs={...})
# or
app = FastAPI()
```

**Exception Handlers:**
```python
app = FastAPI(
    exception_handlers={
        HTTPException: http_exception_handler,
        RequestValidationError: request_validation_exception_handler,
        Exception: server_error_exception_handler,
    }
)
```

### 9. EXISTING TEST FILE: test_rate_limiter.py

**Location:** `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py`

**Structure:**
- Uses pytest class-based organization: `class TestRateLimitMiddleware`
- Fixture pattern for app and client setup
- Tests cover:
  - Rate limit enforcement (burst vs steady state)
  - Header presence and values (X-RateLimit-*)
  - 429 response format with retry_after
  - Per-IP rate limiting
  - Cross-path limit sharing
  - Custom configuration

**Test Methods:**
1. `test_allows_requests_under_limit()` - Burst requests
2. `test_blocks_requests_over_burst()` - Rate limit enforcement
3. `test_rate_limit_headers_present()` - Header validation
4. `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases()` - Header changes
5. `test_rate_limit_per_ip()` - IP-based limiting
6. `test_different_paths_share_limit()` - Cross-path limits
7. `test_429_response_format()` - Error response structure
8. `test_custom_limits()` - Configuration handling

### 10. TESTING UTILITIES & HELPERS

**Common Test Utilities Used:**
- `dirty-equals` - Flexible assertion matching
- `inline-snapshot` - Snapshot testing
- `pytest-xdist` - Parallel test execution
- `pytest-cov` - Coverage reporting

**Assertion Patterns:**
```python
from dirty_equals import AnyThing, IsOneOf, IsPartialDict

assert response.json() == {
    "detail": [
        {
            "type": "missing",
            "loc": ["header", "p"],
            "msg": "Field required",
            "input": AnyThing,  # Matches any value
        }
    ]
}
```

### 11. KEY FILES FOR RATE LIMIT TESTING

**Direct paths:**
- Test file: `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py`
- Middleware template: `/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py`
- TestClient examples: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`
- Header testing: `/tests/test_request_params/test_header/test_required_str.py`
- Response header testing: `/tests/test_repeated_cookie_headers.py`

### 12. SUMMARY OF TEST PATTERNS FOR RATE LIMITING MIDDLEWARE

**Essential Elements:**
1. **Fixture Management**: Use `@pytest.fixture` for app and client setup
2. **Class-Based Organization**: Group related tests in test classes
3. **Header Testing**: Access via `response.headers["header-name"]`
4. **Status Codes**: Verify with `response.status_code`
5. **Response Validation**: Parse JSON with `response.json()`
6. **Middleware Registration**: Use `app.add_middleware()`
7. **Custom Logic**: Implement as ASGI middleware with `__call__` method
8. **Exception Handling**: Return proper JSON error responses

**Testing Approach:**
- Test under-limit requests (success cases)
- Test over-limit requests (429 responses)
- Verify header presence and values
- Test reset/decay behavior
- Test per-identifier (IP) separation
- Test custom configuration options
- Test response format compliance
- Test edge cases (boundary conditions)

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

**Middleware Export Pattern:**
Most FastAPI middleware are simple re-exports from Starlette using `from starlette.middleware.X import Y as Y` with `# noqa` comment.

**Custom Middleware Example (`AsyncExitStackMiddleware`):**
```python
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

**Middleware Registration:**
- Uses `app.add_middleware()` method (inherited from Starlette)
- Called like: `app.add_middleware(MiddlewareClass, param1=value1, param2=value2)`
- Middleware are processed in reverse order (last added is outermost)

---

### 4. **Middleware Building System**

**Location:** In `applications.py` - `build_middleware_stack()` method

**How It Works:**
```python
def build_middleware_stack(self) -> ASGIApp:
    # Builds the middleware stack in this order:
    middleware = [
        Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug),
        # ... user_middleware inserted here ...
        Middleware(ExceptionMiddleware, handlers=exception_handlers, debug=debug),
        Middleware(AsyncExitStackMiddleware),
    ]
    
    # Process in reverse (last added is outermost)
    app = self.router
    for cls, args, kwargs in reversed(middleware):
        app = cls(app, *args, **kwargs)
    return app
```

**Middleware Wrapping Order (outermost to innermost):**
1. AsyncExitStackMiddleware
2. ExceptionMiddleware
3. User middleware (in reverse order added)
4. ServerErrorMiddleware
5. Router (actual request handling)

from starlette.requests import Request

- `request.headers` - Headers dict
- `request.query_params` - Query string params
- `request.path` - URL path
- `request.client` - Tuple of (host, port) or None
  - Access IP: `request.client.host` if `request.client` is not None
- `request.scope` - ASGI scope dict

from starlette.responses import Response
```

### 10. **Important Patterns to Follow**

**Middleware Pattern (ASGI-style):**
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class MyMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, param: str = "default") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.param = param

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        
        # Do something before
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        # Do something after
```

**Exception Handling Pattern:**
```python
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse

response = JSONResponse(
    status_code=429,
    content={"detail": "message", "retry_after": 60}
)
await send({
    "type": "http.response.start",
    "status": 429,
    "headers": [(b"content-type", b"application/json")],
})
await send({
    "type": "http.response.body",
    "body": json_str.encode(),
})
```

**Test Pattern:**
- Create app with middleware via `app.add_middleware()`
- Use `TestClient(app)` for HTTP requests
- Assert on `response.status_code`, `response.headers`, `response.json()`

- All existing middleware are thin wrappers around Starlette middleware
- Custom FastAPI middleware follow ASGI conventions
- Comprehensive test suite with pytest

- No built-in rate limiting yet (the test file shows expectations)

- Standard pattern: async ASGI middleware with `__init__` and `__call__` methods

- **mypy**: Strict mode enabled, with pydantic plugin
- **ruff (linting)**: E, W, F, I, B, C4, UP rules
- **coverage**: Parallel testing support, source includes docs_src, tests, fastapi

**Python Version Support:** 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, 3.14

---

### 8. **Key Dependencies**

**Direct Dependencies:**
1. **starlette (>=0.46.0)** - ASGI framework, provides:
   - Base middleware classes
   - Request/Response objects
   - Routing system (extended by FastAPI)
   - Middleware utilities (BaseHTTPMiddleware, Middleware)

2. **pydantic (>=2.9.0)** - Data validation:
   - Request body validation
   - Response serialization
   - Field definitions

3. **typing-extensions (>=4.8.0)** - Type hints backports

4. **typing-inspection (>=0.4.2)** - Type hint introspection

5. **annotated-doc (>=0.0.2)** - Documentation from Annotated types

**Test Dependencies:**
- httpx - HTTP test client
- pytest - Test framework
- anyio[trio] - Async I/O testing
- dirty-equals - Flexible test assertions
- inline-snapshot - Snapshot testing

**Important Note:** No Redis or external caching is in core dependencies. Rate limiting would need to use in-memory data structures.

---

### 9. **Request/Response Handling**

**Request Object:** FastAPI re-exports from Starlette
```python
from starlette.requests import Request
```

**Available on Request:**
- `request.method` - HTTP method
- `request.url` - Full URL
- `request.headers` - Headers dict
- `request.query_params` - Query string params
- `request.path` - URL path
- `request.client` - Tuple of (host, port) or None
  - Access IP: `request.client.host` if `request.client` is not None
- `request.scope` - ASGI scope dict

**Response Object:**
```python
from starlette.responses import Response
```

---

### 10. **Important Patterns to Follow**

**Middleware Pattern (ASGI-style):**
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class MyMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, param: str = "default") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.param = param

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return
        
        # Do something before
        await self.app(scope, receive, send)
        # Do something after
```

**Exception Handling Pattern:**
```python
from fastapi.exceptions import HTTPException
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse

response = JSONResponse(
    status_code=429,
    content={"detail": "message", "retry_after": 60}
)
await send({
    "type": "http.response.start",
    "status": 429,
    "headers": [(b"content-type", b"application/json")],
})
await send({
    "type": "http.response.body",
    "body": json_str.encode(),
})
```

**Test Pattern:**
- Create app with middleware via `app.add_middleware()`
- Use `TestClient(app)` for HTTP requests
- Assert on `response.status_code`, `response.headers`, `response.json()`

---

### Summary

The FastAPI codebase is well-structured with:
- Clean separation between framework code and middleware
- All existing middleware are thin wrappers around Starlette middleware
- Custom FastAPI middleware follow ASGI conventions
- Comprehensive test suite with pytest
- Clear middleware registration and building pipeline
- No built-in rate limiting yet (the test file shows expectations)
- Request IP available via `request.client.host` with null safety needed
- Standard pattern: async ASGI middleware with `__init__` and `__call__` methods

The test_rate_limiter.py file already exists with full test expectations, showing what needs to be implemented.

import time
import pytest
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )

    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"message": "hello"}

    @app.get("/other")
    async def other():
        return {"message": "other"}

    return app


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)


class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
        for _ in range(5):
            response = client.get("/")
            assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
        # Use up the burst
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        # Next request should be rate limited
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
        assert "retry_after" in body

    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
        response = client.get("/")
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers

    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
        r1 = client.get("/")
        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        r2 = client.get("/")
        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        assert remaining2 < remaining1

    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"

    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
        # Use up burst across different endpoints
        for _ in range(3):
            client.get("/")
        for _ in range(2):
            client.get("/other")
        # Should be rate limited now
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429

    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
        assert body["retry_after"] > 0

    def test_custom_limits(self):
        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
        app = FastAPI()
        app.add_middleware(
            RateLimitMiddleware,
            requests_per_minute=120,
            burst_size=2,
        )

        @app.get("/")
        async def root():
            return {"ok": True}

        client = TestClient(app)
        # Burst of 2
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

from contextlib import AsyncExitStack

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
# in their own AsyncExitStack
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

Now I have all the information I need to create a detailed implementation plan. Let me think through the token bucket algorithm and the implementation details.

### Token Bucket Algorithm

The token bucket algorithm works like this:
1. Each client IP has a "bucket" with a maximum capacity of `burst_size` tokens.
2. Tokens are added to the bucket at a rate of `requests_per_minute` tokens per minute.
3. Each incoming request consumes 1 token.
4. If there are no tokens available, the request is rejected with a 429 status.

Implementation:
- Store a dict mapping `client_ip -> (tokens, last_refill_time)`


- On each request, I'll look up or create the bucket for that IP, calculate how many tokens to add based on elapsed time since the last refill, cap it at the burst size, and either allow the request if tokens are available or reject it with a 429 response.

For thread safety with FastAPI's async nature, I'll use `asyncio.Lock` to protect concurrent access to the bucket dictionary, though since this is an in-process in-memory solution, I need to be careful about how Python handles concurrency here.

For the response headers, I'll include `X-RateLimit-Limit` with the requests per minute, `X-RateLimit-Remaining` showing the current token count, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` with a Unix timestamp indicating when the bucket will be full or when the next token becomes available. So `retry_after` needs to be the number of seconds until the next token becomes available, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` should be present in the response headers. Looking at the test expectations, `retry_after` is a positive number representing seconds, while `X-RateLimit-Reset` just needs to exist without a specific format requirement. Now I'm setting up the middleware implementation structure in the rate limit module.

Defining the token bucket class... I'm implementing the token consumption logic that checks if a request is allowed under the rate limit, refilling the bucket based on elapsed time and returning whether the request should be allowed along with retry information.

Now I'm finishing the middleware layer that extracts the client IP, acquires a lock to safely check the token bucket, and prepares the rate limit response headers with the reset time. Writing middleware logic...

Now I'm realizing there's a potential issue with how the async lock is being initialized in the middleware class—I need to think through whether it's properly set up for concurrent requests. I'm realizing that since `_consume_token` is purely synchronous with no awaits, we don't actually need the asyncio.Lock at all—the GIL and asyncio's non-preemptive nature between awaits means it's already atomic. For a single-process in-memory implementation, we can safely remove the lock entirely. I'm realizing that `asyncio.Lock` won't work across different threads since each thread has its own event loop, so I need to use `threading.Lock` instead—it's the right choice for synchronous operations like token consumption and works safely in both sync and async contexts. I'll keep the critical section minimal to avoid blocking the event loop.

For the rate limit headers, I'm thinking through the semantics: `X-RateLimit-Reset` should be a Unix timestamp indicating when the bucket will be full again, and for 429 responses, `retry_after` tells the client how many seconds until they can retry.

Looking at what the tests actually validate, they're checking that the headers exist and that `retry_after` is a positive number, but they don't enforce specific values for the Reset header, which gives me some flexibility in implementation.

Now I need to update the `__init__.py` to export `RateLimitMiddleware`. Based on the test import pattern and how other middleware modules are structured in FastAPI, the test expects to import directly from `fastapi.middleware.rate_limit`, so I don't need to add it to the main `__init__.py` file.

My implementation plan is to create the rate limit middleware using a token bucket algorithm with IP-based state tracking, protect the state with a threading lock for thread safety, and ensure all responses include the required rate limit headers.

input.content:
# Rate Limiting Middleware (Token Bucket) for FastAPI

## Context

The codebase has an existing test file at `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` that imports
`from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware` — but no such module
exists yet. This plan adds a per-client-IP token-bucket rate-limiting middleware that:

- Allows up to `burst_size` requests instantly (burst allowance)
- Refills tokens at a steady `requests_per_minute` rate
- Returns HTTP 429 with `detail` ("Rate limit exceeded…") and `retry_after` (seconds)
- Attaches `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers
  on **every** response (200s and 429s alike)
- Is exported from the `fastapi.middleware` package

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| Action | Path |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` |
| **Modify** | `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` |

No other files need changes.

---

## Token Bucket Algorithm

```
On each request for client IP:
  1. Look up (or initialise) the bucket: (tokens=burst_size, last_refill=now)
  2. elapsed = now - last_refill
  3. tokens = min(burst_size, tokens + elapsed * rate_per_second)
  4. last_refill = now
  5. if tokens >= 1.0:
       tokens -= 1.0  →  allow request
     else:
       retry_after = (1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second  →  reject 429
```

`rate_per_second = requests_per_minute / 60.0`

---

## Implementation: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`

```python
import json
import math
import threading
import time

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


class RateLimitMiddleware:
    """
    Per-client-IP token-bucket rate limiting middleware.

    Args:
        app: The ASGI application to wrap.
        requests_per_minute: Steady-state refill rate (tokens/minute).
        burst_size: Bucket capacity — max instantaneous burst.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        app: ASGIApp,
        requests_per_minute: int = 60,
        burst_size: int = 10,
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.requests_per_minute = requests_per_minute
        self.burst_size = burst_size
        self._rate: float = requests_per_minute / 60.0          # tokens/second
        # bucket state: ip -> [tokens: float, last_refill: float]
        self._buckets: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def _get_client_ip(self, scope: Scope) -> str:
        """Return client IP, preferring X-Forwarded-For."""
        headers: dict[bytes, bytes] = dict(scope.get("headers", []))
        xff = headers.get(b"x-forwarded-for")
        if xff:
            return xff.decode().split(",")[0].strip()
        client = scope.get("client")
        if client:
            return client[0]
        return "unknown"

    def _consume(self, ip: str) -> tuple[bool, int, float]:
        """
        Attempt to consume one token.

        Returns:
            (allowed, remaining_tokens_floor, seconds_until_reset)
        """
        now = time.monotonic()
        with self._lock:
            if ip not in self._buckets:
                self._buckets[ip] = [float(self.burst_size), now]

            bucket = self._buckets[ip]
            elapsed = now - bucket[1]
            bucket[0] = min(self.burst_size, bucket[0] + elapsed * self._rate)
            bucket[1] = now

            if bucket[0] >= 1.0:
                bucket[0] -= 1.0
                remaining = math.floor(bucket[0])
                # Seconds until bucket is completely full again
                seconds_to_full = (self.burst_size - bucket[0]) / self._rate
                return True, remaining, seconds_to_full
            else:
                # Seconds until one more token arrives
                retry_after = (1.0 - bucket[0]) / self._rate
                return False, 0, retry_after

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        client_ip = self._get_client_ip(scope)
        allowed, remaining, time_value = self._consume(client_ip)
        reset_ts = int(time.time() + time_value)

        if not allowed:
            retry_after = round(time_value, 3)
            body = json.dumps(
                {"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.",
                 "retry_after": retry_after}
            ).encode()
            await send({
                "type": "http.response.start",
                "status": 429,
                "headers": [
                    (b"content-type", b"application/json"),
                    (b"content-length", str(len(body)).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-limit", str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", b"0"),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-reset", str(reset_ts).encode()),
                ],
            })
            await send({"type": "http.response.body", "body": body})
            return

        # Inject headers into the downstream response
        rl_headers = [
            (b"x-ratelimit-limit",     str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", str(remaining).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-reset",     str(reset_ts).encode()),
        ]

        async def send_with_rl_headers(message: dict) -> None:
            if message["type"] == "http.response.start":
                headers = list(message.get("headers", []))
                headers.extend(rl_headers)
                message = {**message, "headers": headers}
            await send(message)

        await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_rl_headers)
```

### Key decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| `threading.Lock` (not `asyncio.Lock`) | Works safely in both sync (TestClient) and async contexts; the critical section is pure in-memory arithmetic so the lock is held for < 1 µs |
| `list[float]` for bucket state | Mutable in-place; avoids dict re-insertion on each refill |
| `math.floor` for `remaining` | Matches the "whole requests remaining" semantic users expect |
| Direct ASGI `send` for 429 | Bypasses the downstream app entirely; no partial response can leak |
| `send_with_rl_headers` closure | Appends headers once, on the single `http.response.start` message |
| `time.monotonic()` for bucket | Monotonic avoids clock-skew issues; `time.time()` for `Reset` header (absolute epoch expected by clients) |

---

## Modification: `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

Add one line following the existing `Middleware` re-export:

```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware

from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware as RateLimitMiddleware
```

---

## Header Semantics

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | `requests_per_minute` (the configured rate) |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | `floor(tokens)` after consuming (0 on 429) |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp (int) when bucket is full / next token arrives |

On 429 responses the body also carries:

```json
{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.", "retry_after": 1.234}
```

---

## How the Tests Will Pass

| Test | Why it passes |
|------|---------------|
| `test_allows_requests_under_limit` | burst_size=5 → first 5 requests each have a token available |
| `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` | 6th request finds tokens < 1 → 429 with correct body |
| `test_rate_limit_headers_present` | `send_with_rl_headers` always appends 3 headers |
| `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` | `remaining` decreases by 1 each call |
| `test_rate_limit_per_ip` | `X-RateLimit-Limit` == str(requests_per_minute) == "60" |
| `test_different_paths_share_limit` | bucket is keyed only by IP, not path |
| `test_429_response_format` | body is `{"detail": str, "retry_after": float > 0}` |
| `test_custom_limits` | burst_size=2 → third request blocked |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Run only the new test file
pytest tests/test_rate_limiter.py -v

# Run the full test suite (must stay green)
pytest --timeout=20 -x -q

# Optional: type-check the new file
mypy fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py --strict
```

- Refills tokens at a steady `requests_per_minute` rate
- Returns HTTP 429 with `detail` ("Rate limit exceeded…") and `retry_after` (seconds)

## Token Bucket Algorithm

```
On each request for client IP:
  1. Look up (or initialise) the bucket: (tokens=burst_size, last_refill=now)

import threading

        """Return client IP, preferring X-Forwarded-For."""

        if xff:

        client = scope.get("client")

            return client[0]
        return "unknown"

    def _consume(self, ip: str) -> tuple[bool, int, float]:
        """
        Attempt to consume one token.

            (allowed, remaining_tokens_floor, seconds_until_reset)
        """
        now = time.monotonic()

                return True, remaining, seconds_to_full
            else:

                retry_after = (1.0 - bucket[0]) / self._rate
                return False, 0, retry_after

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        client_ip = self._get_client_ip(scope)
        allowed, remaining, time_value = self._consume(client_ip)
        reset_ts = int(time.time() + time_value)

        if not allowed:
            retry_after = round(time_value, 3)
            body = json.dumps(
                {"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.",
                 "retry_after": retry_after}
            ).encode()
            await send({
                "type": "http.response.start",
                "status": 429,
                "headers": [
                    (b"content-type", b"application/json"),
                    (b"content-length", str(len(body)).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-limit", str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", b"0"),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-reset", str(reset_ts).encode()),

            (b"x-ratelimit-limit",     str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", str(remaining).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-reset",     str(reset_ts).encode()),

        await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_rl_headers)

## Implementation Plan: Token Bucket Rate Limiting Middleware

### What needs to be done

**2 files total** — one new, one small edit:

1. **Create `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`** — the full middleware implementation
2. **Edit `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** — add one re-export line

---

### How the algorithm works

Each client IP gets a "token bucket":
- Starts full at `burst_size` tokens
- Refills continuously at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens/second
- Each request consumes 1 token → allowed if available, 429 if not

---

### Key implementation details

| Concern | Approach |
|---------|----------|
| Thread safety | `threading.Lock` (works in both sync TestClient and async runtime; held for < 1 µs) |
| IP extraction | Check `x-forwarded-for` header first, fall back to `scope["client"][0]` |
| Headers on 200 | Wrap the downstream `send` callable to inject 3 headers at `http.response.start` |
| Headers on 429 | Short-circuit before calling `self.app`; send full ASGI response directly |
| `retry_after` | `(1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second` — seconds until next token |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | `int(time.time() + seconds_until_reset)` — absolute Unix timestamp |
| Monotonic time | Use `time.monotonic()` for bucket arithmetic to avoid clock skew |

---

### All 8 existing tests will pass

The test file already exists at `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` with expectations that precisely match this design (burst=5, 429 body with `detail`/`retry_after`, all 3 `X-RateLimit-*` headers present on every response, per-IP isolation, path-agnostic limits).

## Implementation Plan

Here's the full plan — **2 files, zero new dependencies**.

---

### Files

| Action | Path |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` |
| **Edit** | `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` (1 line added) |

---

### Token Bucket Algorithm

```
Per request for a client IP:
  1. Look up (or create) bucket: {tokens=burst_size, last_refill=now}
  2. elapsed = now - last_refill
  3. tokens = min(burst_size,  tokens + elapsed × rate_per_second)
  4. last_refill = now
  5. if tokens ≥ 1.0 → consume 1 token → allow
     else            → reject 429, retry_after = (1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second
```

---

### Key Design Decisions

| Concern | Decision & Why |
|---------|----------------|
| **Thread safety** | `threading.Lock` — works in both sync TestClient and async runtime; the critical section is pure arithmetic held for < 1 µs |
| **IP extraction** | Check `x-forwarded-for` header first, fall back to `scope["client"][0]`, then `"unknown"` |
| **Headers on 200** | Wrap the downstream `send` callable to inject 3 `X-RateLimit-*` headers at the `http.response.start` message |
| **Headers on 429** | Short-circuit before calling `self.app`; send ASGI response directly |
| **`retry_after`** | `(1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second` seconds, rounded to 3 decimal places |
| **`X-RateLimit-Reset`** | `int(time.time() + seconds)` — absolute Unix epoch timestamp |
| **Time source** | `time.monotonic()` for bucket arithmetic (avoids clock skew); `time.time()` only for the Reset header |

---

### How Every Existing Test Passes

| Test | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `test_allows_requests_under_limit` | `burst_size=5` → first 5 requests always have a token |
| `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` | 6th request finds `tokens < 1` → 429 + correct body |
| `test_rate_limit_headers_present` | `send_with_rl_headers` always appends all 3 headers |
| `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` | `floor(tokens)` decreases by 1 each call |
| `test_rate_limit_per_ip` | `X-RateLimit-Limit == "60"` (the configured `requests_per_minute`) |
| `test_different_paths_share_limit` | Bucket keyed by IP only, not path |
| `test_429_response_format` | Body is `{"detail": str, "retry_after": float > 0}` |
| `test_custom_limits` | `burst_size=2` → 3rd request blocked |

---

### Verification

```bash
pytest tests/test_rate_limiter.py -v   # new tests
pytest --timeout=20 -x -q              # full suite (must stay green)
mypy fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py --strict   # optional type check
```

## Implementation Plan: Token Bucket Rate Limiting Middleware

### What needs to be done

**2 files total** — one new, one small edit:

1. **Create `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`** — the full middleware implementation
2. **Edit `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`** — add one re-export line

---

### How the algorithm works

Each client IP gets a "token bucket":
- Starts full at `burst_size` tokens
- Refills continuously at `requests_per_minute / 60` tokens/second
- Each request consumes 1 token → allowed if available, 429 if not

---

### Key implementation details

| Concern | Approach |
|---------|----------|
| Thread safety | `threading.Lock` (works in both sync TestClient and async runtime; held for < 1 µs) |
| IP extraction | Check `x-forwarded-for` header first, fall back to `scope["client"][0]` |
| Headers on 200 | Wrap the downstream `send` callable to inject 3 headers at `http.response.start` |
| Headers on 429 | Short-circuit before calling `self.app`; send full ASGI response directly |
| `retry_after` | `(1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second` — seconds until next token |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | `int(time.time() + seconds_until_reset)` — absolute Unix timestamp |
| Monotonic time | Use `time.monotonic()` for bucket arithmetic to avoid clock skew |

---

### All 8 existing tests will pass

The test file already exists at `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` with expectations that precisely match this design (burst=5, 429 body with `detail`/`retry_after`, all 3 `X-RateLimit-*` headers present on every response, per-IP isolation, path-agnostic limits).
tool_input.plan:
# Rate Limiting Middleware (Token Bucket) for FastAPI

## Context

The codebase has an existing test file at `tests/test_rate_limiter.py` that imports
`from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware` — but no such module
exists yet. This plan adds a per-client-IP token-bucket rate-limiting middleware that:

- Allows up to `burst_size` requests instantly (burst allowance)
- Refills tokens at a steady `requests_per_minute` rate
- Returns HTTP 429 with `detail` ("Rate limit exceeded…") and `retry_after` (seconds)
- Attaches `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers
  on **every** response (200s and 429s alike)
- Is exported from the `fastapi.middleware` package

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| Action | Path |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py` |
| **Modify** | `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py` |

No other files need changes.

---

## Token Bucket Algorithm

```
On each request for client IP:
  1. Look up (or initialise) the bucket: (tokens=burst_size, last_refill=now)
  2. elapsed = now - last_refill
  3. tokens = min(burst_size, tokens + elapsed * rate_per_second)
  4. last_refill = now
  5. if tokens >= 1.0:
       tokens -= 1.0  →  allow request
     else:
       retry_after = (1.0 - tokens) / rate_per_second  →  reject 429
```

`rate_per_second = requests_per_minute / 60.0`

---

## Implementation: `fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`

```python
import json
import math
import threading
import time

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


class RateLimitMiddleware:
    """
    Per-client-IP token-bucket rate limiting middleware.

    Args:
        app: The ASGI application to wrap.
        requests_per_minute: Steady-state refill rate (tokens/minute).
        burst_size: Bucket capacity — max instantaneous burst.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        app: ASGIApp,
        requests_per_minute: int = 60,
        burst_size: int = 10,
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.requests_per_minute = requests_per_minute
        self.burst_size = burst_size
        self._rate: float = requests_per_minute / 60.0          # tokens/second
        # bucket state: ip -> [tokens: float, last_refill: float]
        self._buckets: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def _get_client_ip(self, scope: Scope) -> str:
        """Return client IP, preferring X-Forwarded-For."""
        headers: dict[bytes, bytes] = dict(scope.get("headers", []))
        xff = headers.get(b"x-forwarded-for")
        if xff:
            return xff.decode().split(",")[0].strip()
        client = scope.get("client")
        if client:
            return client[0]
        return "unknown"

    def _consume(self, ip: str) -> tuple[bool, int, float]:
        """
        Attempt to consume one token.

        Returns:
            (allowed, remaining_tokens_floor, seconds_until_reset)
        """
        now = time.monotonic()
        with self._lock:
            if ip not in self._buckets:
                self._buckets[ip] = [float(self.burst_size), now]

            bucket = self._buckets[ip]
            elapsed = now - bucket[1]
            bucket[0] = min(self.burst_size, bucket[0] + elapsed * self._rate)
            bucket[1] = now

            if bucket[0] >= 1.0:
                bucket[0] -= 1.0
                remaining = math.floor(bucket[0])
                # Seconds until bucket is completely full again
                seconds_to_full = (self.burst_size - bucket[0]) / self._rate
                return True, remaining, seconds_to_full
            else:
                # Seconds until one more token arrives
                retry_after = (1.0 - bucket[0]) / self._rate
                return False, 0, retry_after

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        if scope["type"] != "http":
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
            return

        client_ip = self._get_client_ip(scope)
        allowed, remaining, time_value = self._consume(client_ip)
        reset_ts = int(time.time() + time_value)

        if not allowed:
            retry_after = round(time_value, 3)
            body = json.dumps(
                {"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.",
                 "retry_after": retry_after}
            ).encode()
            await send({
                "type": "http.response.start",
                "status": 429,
                "headers": [
                    (b"content-type", b"application/json"),
                    (b"content-length", str(len(body)).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-limit", str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", b"0"),
                    (b"x-ratelimit-reset", str(reset_ts).encode()),
                ],
            })
            await send({"type": "http.response.body", "body": body})
            return

        # Inject headers into the downstream response
        rl_headers = [
            (b"x-ratelimit-limit",     str(self.requests_per_minute).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-remaining", str(remaining).encode()),
            (b"x-ratelimit-reset",     str(reset_ts).encode()),
        ]

        async def send_with_rl_headers(message: dict) -> None:
            if message["type"] == "http.response.start":
                headers = list(message.get("headers", []))
                headers.extend(rl_headers)
                message = {**message, "headers": headers}
            await send(message)

        await self.app(scope, receive, send_with_rl_headers)
```

### Key decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| `threading.Lock` (not `asyncio.Lock`) | Works safely in both sync (TestClient) and async contexts; the critical section is pure in-memory arithmetic so the lock is held for < 1 µs |
| `list[float]` for bucket state | Mutable in-place; avoids dict re-insertion on each refill |
| `math.floor` for `remaining` | Matches the "whole requests remaining" semantic users expect |
| Direct ASGI `send` for 429 | Bypasses the downstream app entirely; no partial response can leak |
| `send_with_rl_headers` closure | Appends headers once, on the single `http.response.start` message |
| `time.monotonic()` for bucket | Monotonic avoids clock-skew issues; `time.time()` for `Reset` header (absolute epoch expected by clients) |

---

## Modification: `fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

Add one line following the existing `Middleware` re-export:

```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware

from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware as RateLimitMiddleware
```

---

## Header Semantics

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | `requests_per_minute` (the configured rate) |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | `floor(tokens)` after consuming (0 on 429) |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp (int) when bucket is full / next token arrives |

On 429 responses the body also carries:

```json
{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.", "retry_after": 1.234}
```

---

## How the Tests Will Pass

| Test | Why it passes |
|------|---------------|
| `test_allows_requests_under_limit` | burst_size=5 → first 5 requests each have a token available |
| `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` | 6th request finds tokens < 1 → 429 with correct body |
| `test_rate_limit_headers_present` | `send_with_rl_headers` always appends 3 headers |
| `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` | `remaining` decreases by 1 each call |
| `test_rate_limit_per_ip` | `X-RateLimit-Limit` == str(requests_per_minute) == "60" |
| `test_different_paths_share_limit` | bucket is keyed only by IP, not path |
| `test_429_response_format` | body is `{"detail": str, "retry_after": float > 0}` |
| `test_custom_limits` | burst_size=2 → third request blocked |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Run only the new test file
pytest tests/test_rate_limiter.py -v

# Run the full test suite (must stay green)
pytest --timeout=20 -x -q

# Optional: type-check the new file
mypy fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py --strict
```

1	import time
2	import pytest
3	from fastapi import FastAPI
4	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
5	from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware
6	
7	
8	@pytest.fixture
9	def app():
10	    app = FastAPI()
11	    app.add_middleware(
12	        RateLimitMiddleware,
13	        requests_per_minute=60,
14	        burst_size=5,
15	    )
16	
17	    @app.get("/")
18	    async def root():
19	        return {"message": "hello"}
20	
21	    @app.get("/other")
22	    async def other():
23	        return {"message": "other"}
24	
25	    return app
26	
27	
28	@pytest.fixture
29	def client(app):
30	    return TestClient(app)
31	
32	
33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
